data.content: The project uses **MSTest v3.8.0** with Microsoft Testing Platform enabled (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`). I'll run only the Integration-category tests.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run integration tests with dotnet test

data.reasoningText:
The project uses **MSTest** (v3.8.0) with `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. The `Directory.Build.props` has `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` set to `true`, which means it uses the newer Microsoft Testing Platform integration with `dotnet test`.

The tests have `TestCategory` attributes — "Unit" and "Integration". I need to run only the Integration tests.

With MSTest and the testing platform, to filter by category I can use `--filter "TestCategory=Integration"`.

arguments.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-944ec5ed4ffd4340bac89e0f74503e92 && dotnet test --filter \"TestCategory=Integration\" 2>&1", "description": "Run integration tests with dotnet test", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

data.content:
All done! The project uses **MSTest v3.8.0** with the new Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`). The integration test (`GetAll_QueriesDatabase`) passed successfully:

```
dotnet test --filter "TestCategory=Integration"
```

Build and test succeeded in ~7.2s — 1 integration test ran and passed.
